tests.name: No partial fences
tests.evidence:
Input: "```js
code
```" -> Chunks: ["```js
code
```"]. Single chunk with fences intact, NOT split at fence boundaries.

tests.name: CHUNK_DELAY_MS = 100
tests.evidence: CHUNK_DELAY_MS = 100 defined at line 363 in relay.ts. Correctly implements Cursor pattern for spacing Discord messages.

tests.name: Rate limiting implementation
tests.evidence: sendChunksWithRateLimit() uses 'await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, CHUNK_DELAY_MS))' between chunks (relay.ts:371-373)

tests.name: Long code blocks split safely
tests.evidence: Long code block (3010 chars) split into 4 chunks. First chunk has opening fence (```js), last chunk has closing fence (```).

tests.name: sendChunksWithRateLimit uses CHUNK_DELAY_MS
tests.evidence: Verified: for loop iterates chunks, awaits message.reply(chunk), then awaits setTimeout(resolve, CHUNK_DELAY_MS) between chunks.

verdict: Implementation is REAL and WORKING. Code fence aware chunking prevents the flash/freeze bug by keeping ``` boundaries intact during chunking. Rate limiting is properly implemented with 100ms delays. The core protection against code block markdown confusion is in place.
